channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Two predawn explosions rock the area around the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen but no one is injured
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Police in the Danish capital said two predawn explosions took place Wednesday in the vicinity of the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, prompting the nearby Jewish school to close for the day.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Palestinian officials say 32 killed in Israeli strikes on southern Gaza
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes killed at least 32 people in southern Gaza overnight, mostly women and children, as the military launched ground operations in the hard-hit city of Khan Younis, Palestinian medical officials said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thai police arrest driver and work to identify victims of school bus fire that killed 23
item.description: Police have arrested the driver of a bus carrying young students and teachers that caught fire and killed 23 in suburban Bangkok, as families arrived to help identify their loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China marks 75 years of Communist Party rule as economic challenges and security threats linger
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China is marking the 75th year of Communist Party rule as economic challenges and security threats linger over the massive state.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Christmas starts in Venezuela, but the festive mood is nowhere to be found
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — It doesn’t feel like Christmas in Venezuela. At least not yet. Even if the president wants it to be.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO and JUAN PABLO ARRAEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel to have 'substantial response' to Iran attacks: John Bolton
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, less than a day after Israel began ground attacks on the militant group Hezbollah in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: A plane carrying Brazil President Lula lands safely at Mexico City airport after circling for hours
item.description: ZUMPANGO, Mexico (AP) — An airplane carrying Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva back to Brasilia landed safely on Tuesday at Mexico City’s Felipe Angeles airport after circling for hours due to a technical problem.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE and MARTIN SILVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran attacks ratchet up Middle East conflict: 5 takeaways
item.description: The Middle East is a powder keg after Iran launched nearly 200 missiles toward Israel Wednesday, with the United States vowing "severe consequences" for the action. No deaths in Israel were immediately assessed, and U.S. officials said the missiles — aimed at Israel and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump warns of 'global catastrophe' following Iranian missile attack on Israel
item.description: Former President Trump on Tuesday bashed President Biden and Vice President Harris in the wake of an Iranian missile attack on Israel, arguing if Harris is elected in November, it would lead to mass chaos on the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: White House calls Iranian missile attack 'significant escalation'
item.description: White House national security adviser Jake Sullivan on Tuesday called Iran's missile attack against Israel a "significant escalation" and said the Biden administration is determining how to respond.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: What to know about Hezbollah's capabilities after its recent losses
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah has suffered some of the heaviest losses in its history over the past two weeks, chief among them the killing of its longtime leader, Hassan Nasrallah, in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico's Claudia Sheinbaum sworn in as North America's first female head of state
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum was sworn in at a joint session of the country’s Congress on Tuesday, becoming North America’s first female head of state.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran fired dozens of missiles into Israel on Tuesday, prompting Israel to order residents to remain close to bomb shelters, while the U.S. warned of severe consequences if Iran launched a ballistic missile against Israel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: IDF meets little resistance from Hezbollah after weeks of hitting terror targets, officials say
item.description: The Israel Defense Force met little resistance from Hezbollah terrorists despite weeks of growing anticipation of an Israeli incursion amid strikes and raids by specials ops units in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO's new chief makes Ukraine support a top priority and says he will work with any US president
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The new head of NATO vowed on Tuesday to help shore up Western support for war-ravaged Ukraine and expressed confidence that he can work with whoever is elected president of the United States, the alliance's most powerful member, in November.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: WikiLeaks' Julian Assange says he pleaded 'guilty to journalism' in order to be freed
item.description: STRASBOURG, France (AP) — WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange said Tuesday that he was freed after years of incarceration because he "pled guilty to journalism.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL BASTIEN, BARBARA SURK and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Iran's missile barrage and Israel's ground operations in Lebanon
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles at Israel, though Israeli authorities said there were no injuries and scattered damage and fires from falling shrapnel.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korea unveils its most powerful missile, which could reach North Korea's underground bunkers
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea unveiled its most powerful ballistic missile and other weapons that could target North Korea during a massive Armed Forces Day ceremony on Tuesday, as the South's president warned the North's regime would collapse if it attempts to use nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A cruise ship bound on a round-the-world voyage leaves Belfast after a 4-month delay
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A cruise ship taking passengers on a 3 ½-year around-the-world voyage has finally sailed from Belfast's dockside, after spending four months stuck in the Northern Ireland capital.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian fighter jet intercepted near Alaska by NORAD forces after ‘unsafe, unprofessional’ interaction
item.description: North American Aerospace Defense Command (NORAD) released a video showing the dangerous moment a Russian Su-35 fighter pilot flew right in front of a NORAD F-16 fighter pilot over the Alaska Air Defense Zone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Flooding, landslides kill nearly 200 in Nepal
item.description: At least 193 people have been confirmed dead following severe flooding and landslides in Nepal, where heavy weekend rainfall was reported.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli raids against Hezbollah signal a wider ground incursion into Lebanon could come soon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military launched small ground raids against Hezbollah and sealed off communities along its northern border on Monday as Israeli artillery pounded southern Lebanon and signals grew that more forces could soon be sent across the border to fight the Iran-backed militants.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, SAMY MAGDY, ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Death toll rises to 18 in South Africa mass shootings, police say
item.description: The death toll from mass shootings at two houses on the same street in a South African village over the weekend has risen to 18, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's ground invasion into Lebanon imminent as cabinet approves next phase of the war
item.description: President Biden demanded a 'ceasefire now' following reports that Washington had been briefed by Israel on its plans to launch a 'limited' ground invasion into Lebanon imminently in fight with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah says it will choose Nasrallah's successor 'at the earliest opportunity'
item.description: Hezbollah’s second-in-command, deputy leader Naim Qassem, says the terrorist group will choose its new leader "at the earliest opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strike kills Hamas commander in Lebanon, 3 Palestinian militant leaders killed in separate strike
item.description: Fateh Sherif, the head of the Lebanon branch of the Palestinian terrorist group Hamas, has been killed overnight in an airstrike, Israeli officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden says he will talk to Netanyahu as Israel pummels Sunni terror targets in Beirut
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces reportedly began striking the Lebanese capital of Beirut on Sunday evening after President Biden said he intended to speak with Prime Minister Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lithuanian FM warns Russia can do 'so much damage to its neighbors'
item.description: Lithuania has been one of the most vocal opponents of Russia since Moscow's invasion of Ukraine in 2022. The NATO country has raised its defense spending to over 3.2% annually.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli Air Force strikes Houthi targets in Yemen with 'extensive' operation
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces said the Israeli Air Force struck Houthi terror targets, including power plants and a seaport used to import oil, in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IDF announces death of another senior Hezbollah official following Nasrallah death
item.description: The Israeli military says it killed yet another member of Hezbollah's top command on Sunday, after wiping out leadership in a Friday strike.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European officials dismiss claim world leaders 'are laughing' at Trump, praise his 'strong message'
item.description: Vice President Harris, during September's presidential debate, claimed world leaders were "laughing" at former President Trump. Several foreign officials beg to differ.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iranian operatives charged with hacking Trump's presidential campaign
item.description: The operatives are accused of hacking Trump's campaign and disseminating stolen information to media organizations.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Prince Harry leading initiative to support Southern African youth
item.description: “These young adults are eager and capable of driving meaningful change, yet significant barriers still stand in their way.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Harris blasts proposals for Ukraine to cede territory to Russia during Zelenskyy meeting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With American support for Ukraine at a partisan crossroads, Vice President Kamala Harris on Thursday slammed suggestions that Kyiv should cede territory for the sake of peace with Moscow as “dangerous and unacceptable.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US and allies call for an immediate 21-day cease-fire between Israel and Hezbollah
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S., France and other allies jointly called Wednesday for an immediate 21-day cease-fire to allow for negotiations in the escalating conflict between Israel and Hezbollah that has killed more than 600 people in Lebanon in recent days.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, MATTHEW LEE and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian missiles and drones target Kyiv for 5 hours and hit Ukraine's power grid
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine’s air defenses battled an overnight Russian aerial attack on the capital Kyiv for five hours, officials said Thursday, as missiles and drones again hammered the Ukrainian power grid. The Kyiv attack injured at least two people, Ukraine’s Emergency Service said.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation
